cybersecurity_text1: At Slase Systems, we help companies and institutions build secure, stable, and threat-resilient IT environments. Our cybersecurity services focus on effectively reducing risk, protecting data, and ensuring business continuity.

deep_locust_text1: Deep Locust is an advanced project focused on developing a dedicated artificial intelligence algorithm for controlling quadcopter drones. The system integrates a proprietary AI model with a specialized onboard computer, a configuration platform, and a 3D simulation environment, forming a complete and optimized architecture for operation in complex, dynamic environments.
deep_locust_text2: The project has been designed as a scalable platform enabling both individual autonomy and coordinated multi-drone cooperation within a single intelligent ecosystem.
deep_locust_text3: Deep Locust is an integrated system consisting of:
deep_locust_text4: a dedicated AI algorithm for quadcopter control,
deep_locust_text5: an onboard computer optimized for real-time computation,
deep_locust_text6: a model configuration and optimization platform,
deep_locust_text7: a 3D simulation environment for training and validation,
deep_locust_text11: dynamic adaptation to changing environmental conditions,
deep_locust_text13: optimization of energy consumption and computational resources,
deep_locust_text15: At the core of the project is a proprietary artificial intelligence model specifically designed for multi-rotor drone control.
deep_locust_text18: trajectory prediction and adaptive motion planning,
deep_locust_text21: decision optimization based on sensor data.
deep_locust_text22: The model architecture allows flexible adaptation to various operational scenarios – from precision flights to complex autonomous missions.
deep_locust_text23: A key advantage of Deep Locust is its advanced autonomous inter-drone cooperation system, based on:
deep_locust_text31: adaptation to the loss of a single drone,
deep_locust_text34: As a result, Deep Locust creates an intelligent, self-adaptive swarm operating system (swarm intelligence), where each unit acts autonomously while remaining part of a coherent mission structure.
deep_locust_text35: The AI algorithm is integrated with a specialized onboard computer that:
deep_locust_text37: processes sensor data (IMU, cameras, LIDAR, GNSS),
deep_locust_text39: ensures a secure and stable execution environment.
deep_locust_text40: Tight hardware-software integration enables high performance while maintaining low decision latency.
deep_locust_text41: One of the project’s key strengths is a dedicated platform enabling:
deep_locust_text47: algorithm optimization for specific operational scenarios.
deep_locust_text48: The platform enables continuous development and adaptation of the system without rebuilding the entire architecture.
deep_locust_text49: Deep Locust utilizes an advanced 3D simulation environment that:
deep_locust_text52: generates dynamic obstacles and emergency scenarios,
deep_locust_text53: serves as a training stimulus for the neural network.
deep_locust_text56: training in rare and extreme scenarios,
deep_locust_text59: This approach ensures high versatility and maximum algorithm optimization while minimizing operational risk.
deep_locust_text60: Deep Locust is a comprehensive platform including:
deep_locust_text65: tools for configuration, training, and validation.
deep_locust_text66: Proprietary AI algorithm optimized for drone control.
deep_locust_text73: Deep Locust is a modern autonomous control and cooperation platform for quadcopters, combining artificial intelligence, dedicated computing infrastructure, 3D simulation, and swarm intelligence into a unified, advanced technological ecosystem.

educational_simulation_game_text1: The educational simulation game developed by Slase Systems in cooperation with Flint Systems is an interactive environment that teaches the principles of designing structures for wind turbines in an accessible way. The project combines elements of science, physical simulation, and mechanics known from logic and engineering games.
educational_simulation_game_text2: The objective of the game is to create a stable and efficient wind platform capable of withstanding changing weather conditions while optimally utilizing available components.
educational_simulation_game_text3: The player builds a wind turbine structure using three types of components:
educational_simulation_game_text6: neutral components – connecting and stabilizing the entire structure.
educational_simulation_game_text7: Each element has specific physical properties that influence the structure’s behavior during simulation.
educational_simulation_game_text9: designing the most solid and creative structure possible,
educational_simulation_game_text12: The game promotes engineering thinking, solution optimization, and a creative approach to structural design.
educational_simulation_game_text13: After building the platform, the structure undergoes three stages of weather simulation:
educational_simulation_game_text15: Strong wind – testing resistance to dynamic loads.
educational_simulation_game_text16: Storm – extreme conditions testing the limits of structural durability.
educational_simulation_game_text17: Only surviving all three stages means successfully completing the level.
educational_simulation_game_text18: The game is designed as a tool supporting:
educational_simulation_game_text19: learning the basics of physics and structural statics,
educational_simulation_game_text23: The project can be used in:
educational_simulation_game_text29: The Slase Systems educational game combines science, simulation, and interactive gameplay to create an engaging tool for understanding structural design in wind energy.

osc_application_text1: The OSC Application developed by Slase Systems is a universal tool for managing and controlling simulation stations using the OSC (Open Sound Control) protocol. The solution has been designed with flexibility, modularity, and easy adaptation to various simulation and multimedia environments in mind.
osc_application_text2: The application enables the creation of dedicated control interfaces tailored to specific scenarios and workstation requirements.
osc_application_text3: The system has been designed as:
osc_application_text6: a tool communicating via the OSC protocol,
osc_application_text8: As a result, it can be used in simulators, multimedia installations, and automation systems.
osc_application_text9: The application is available in two main versions:
osc_application_text10: mobile version – for Android and iOS platforms, designed for control via tablet or smartphone,
osc_application_text11: desktop version (.NET) – for use on control computers and operator stations.
osc_application_text12: Both versions maintain functional consistency and can be used simultaneously within a single environment.
osc_application_text13: A key feature of the system is its modular interface design, which enables:
osc_application_text14: creation of control panels tailored to a specific workstation,
osc_application_text15: configuration of buttons, sliders, and control elements,
osc_application_text16: assignment of OSC messages to specific UI components,
osc_application_text17: quick interface layout changes without modifying the application code.
osc_application_text18: This allows one tool to support multiple simulator configurations.
osc_application_text22: can cooperate with any OSC-compatible system,
osc_application_text24: Thanks to the open communication standard, the solution can be applied in a wide range of use cases.
osc_application_text25: The application can be used for:
osc_application_text32: full integration with the OSC protocol,
osc_application_text37: The OSC Application by Slase Systems is a flexible tool enabling centralized and intuitive control of simulation systems based on an open communication standard.
osc_application_title1: OSC Application for Managing Simulation Stations
privacy_policy_text1: This Privacy Policy describes the rules for processing personal data of users of the website (hereinafter referred to as the "Website") in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (GDPR).
privacy_policy_text2: The controller of personal data is the owner of the Website.
privacy_policy_text3: While using the Website, the following data may be processed:
privacy_policy_text4: data voluntarily provided via the contact form (e.g. name, email address, message content),
privacy_policy_text5: technical and statistical data (IP address, browser type, operating system),
privacy_policy_text6: data collected automatically by Google tools (Google Analytics, reCAPTCHA).
privacy_policy_text7: Data submitted via the contact form is processed solely for the purpose of:
privacy_policy_text9: conducting correspondence related to the content of the message.
privacy_policy_text10: Providing data is voluntary but necessary to handle the inquiry.
privacy_policy_text11: The Website uses Google Analytics — an analytical tool provided by Google LLC used to analyze website traffic.
privacy_policy_text12: Google Analytics uses cookies to collect anonymous statistical information such as:
privacy_policy_text16: This data is used solely to improve the operation of the Website.
privacy_policy_text17: The Website uses Google reCAPTCHA to protect the contact form against spam and abuse.
privacy_policy_text18: reCAPTCHA analyzes user behavior automatically (e.g. mouse movements, time spent on the site, IP address).
privacy_policy_text19: Data processing is carried out in accordance with Google's privacy policy.
privacy_policy_text20: The Website uses cookies for the purpose of:
privacy_policy_text24: Users may change cookie settings in their web browser at any time.
privacy_policy_text25: Data may be transferred to third parties only to the extent necessary for the operation of the Website, in particular to Google LLC.
privacy_policy_text26: Data may be processed outside the EU (e.g. in the USA) in accordance with applicable data protection mechanisms.
privacy_policy_text33: lodge a complaint with the Polish Data Protection Authority (UODO).
privacy_policy_text34: The controller reserves the right to update this privacy policy.
projects_text1: is a series of immersive simulators dedicated to space operations, orbital technologies, and the future of space exploration. wampir_text1: WAMPIR is an advanced wide-area radio interferometry system designed for the detection, identification, and classification of space objects and aerial vehicles. The project combines distributed antenna infrastructure, precise time synchronization, proprietary computing facilities, and intelligent signal analysis, creating a modern dual-purpose solution – scientific and operational.
wampir_text2: WAMPIR is an integrated antenna system utilizing radio interferometry for:
wampir_text3: identification of cosmic phenomena and objects,
wampir_text4: detection and classification of aerial vehicles,
wampir_text5: analysis of radio activity over a wide area.
wampir_text9: visualization in the form of radio spectra,
wampir_text10: analysis and automatic identification of signal sources.
wampir_text11: Based on processed data, it is possible to determine, among others:
wampir_text17: predicted movement or landing location (using precise geographic coordinates).
wampir_text18: An integral part of the project is a dedicated computing center, responsible for:
wampir_text19: processing large volumes of data in real time,
wampir_text20: correlation of signals from multiple antenna stations,
wampir_text21: execution of AI-based detection and classification algorithms,
wampir_text24: As a result, WAMPIR operates as an autonomous, high-performance infrastructure capable of continuous operational activity.
wampir_text25: The project is integrated with a proprietary SaaS application (Software as a Service), enabling:
wampir_text27: visualization of events and object trajectories,
wampir_text30: access to historical analyses and reports.
wampir_text31: The SaaS platform allows public institutions, research organizations, and technology partners to utilize the WAMPIR system without maintaining their own data processing infrastructure.
wampir_text33: Development of distributed infrastructure monitoring radio activity across the country. The system enables ephemeris tracking and real-time analysis of dynamic radio phenomena.
wampir_text35: The WAMPIR infrastructure can operate as a passive radar, capable of:
wampir_text40: Multiple antennas operate in strict synchronization using an atomic reference, enabling:
wampir_text45: The system uses modern signal processing algorithms and artificial intelligence for:
wampir_text50: WAMPIR is a complete, integrated solution including:
wampir_text55: SaaS application with customer panel and subscription system,
wampir_text62: Capability to operate as a passive radar.
wampir_text63: WAMPIR (Wide-Area Multi-Purpose InterferometeR) is a next-generation radio interferometry system – combining science, security, computing infrastructure, and modern data analysis within a single, coherent technological platform.
